review_notes: Recoverable rows still exist around reporting mismatch, invalid formula, and dashboard validation pain, but that pocket is smaller than in the earlier Google pass.
The remaining residual pool is already mixed with substantial setup, admin, SQL, connector, and generic platform-support noise.
Several currently kept Google rows already look borderline or low-value for deck-ready claims, which raises false-positive risk for any additional rescue broadening.
representative_recoverable_examples: Erro ao explorar os dados / cannot explore data before creating reports
COALESCE invalid formula in blended tables
Dashboard values not aligning across report surfaces
Date-range or public-share behavior breaking dashboard review
representative_non_recoverable_examples: Feature requests and release-note style announcements
Dataproc / Terraform / generic cloud engineering troubleshooting
Credential, embedding, or permission setup questions

why_no_fix: The strongest repeated Google rescue themes are already covered by the current Google-specific invalid-filter rules.
The remaining false-negative pocket is too mixed with generic setup, access, SQL, API, and engineering noise to justify another safe broadening pass.
Some currently valid Google rows already look borderline or low-value, which means the safer pressure point is precision, not more recall.
A new rescue pass now would likely be cosmetic or would raise false positives more than it improves deck-ready blockers.

tests.notes: No rule change was accepted, so no new invalid-filter regression was added or rerun in this pass.

deck_ready_progress.reason: No safe, repeated Google-only rescue pocket remained that could be widened without materially increasing noise.

next_recommendation.detail: Do not widen Google invalid-filter rescue further. If deck-ready work continues, use the source representativeness and weak-source evidence to choose between deck-ready core policy treatment for supporting sources or a different still-representative source-quality pass.
